Or it could be the fear of continuous pain or the fear of future pain. So let's say, if you sold like... Final expense insurance is like funeral insurance. That's more of a fear of what? Future pain of your family you're leaving behind, right? However, if I can help them, even in that situation, feel pain of maybe a relative who passed away in the past, as an example, and they didn't. Have coverage. They can feel someone else's pain and then have that fear that that could happen to them if they don't change. Purchase what you're offered. I'm just giving you a random industry-specific example. So a lot of people say that the two biggest emotional drivers are pain and pleasure. Pleasure is a distant third.
The two things that cause your prospects to want to change our pain and the fear of continuous and future pain that this could keep going on or could happen in the future— let's say you're selling cyber security— there could be future pain if they don't do anything to protect themselves. Okay. So just really to clarify that, with you now, that matches into something I heard Tony Robbins say one time and I go, 'I like to take it actually a step further than what he said in order to persuade at the highest level to influence to sell This is important. You have to master how to emotionally connect with that prospect where they feel like you understand them, their needs, their wants, their desires, even their fears without buying into their story.
That's really important, okay? Now, we talked a little bit about that in some of our training, so you'll see me go through that quite a bit in this portal. Now, this is important. You can understand somebody. You can understand a problem. and judge them, but if you judge them, they will not buy. They have to feel like you understand them. Okay, you emotionally connect with them. You go there first. And when you master how to do that, because remember, you're committed to mastery, right? You're not a winger, a dabbler, or a know-it-all. You're committed to mastery. That's while you're in this training program and you're developing into the best you can be, okay? So to emotionally connect, we have to go there first without buying into their story.
Remember pain and the fear of future pain. Now. The reason why I'm bringing this up to you is I was at a keynote, a speaking engagement. I remember this. This was probably about maybe six, seven months ago. And a lady came up to me. Me and after she's like, 'I love your presentation. This was amazing. I'm actually one of your new clients. I'm in such and such. I think she said she was in our uh anypq 3.' 0 program or something our mid-level program and she said, I just have a really hard time with asking some of these questions because I feel bad when I'm bringing out their pain. And I'm like, Well, The problem with that concept is if you can't help the prospect, relive their pain of their current situation and their past history, and then have a fear of future pain.
The problem for the prospect is, is they will feel no need to change. And when they feel no need to change, That's why you get tons of objections and that's why they don't buy from you. So in reality, I asked her, Who are you actually hurting? By not helping them relive their pain and have a fear of future pain, you're actually hurting the prospect because now they don't feel a need to change. And now their problems stay the same and nothing ever changes for them. I think that was a revelation in her mind. Then she took off and started crushing it, okay? So always remember, selling is not something you do to people. Remember, selling is something you do what? For people. and doing this for them helps them want to change, okay? That's the important part here. Thanks for watching! Thanks for watching!
